steps.title: Use base area times height
steps.content: For a right square prism, $V=s^2h$. The square base has edge length $8$, so its area is $8^2=64$ square units.

steps.title: Set up the volume formula for a right prism
steps.content:
For any prism, Volume = Base Area × Height. The base is a square with edge length 8, so:
$$\text{Base Area} = 8^2 = 64 \text{ square units}$$
Substituting into the volume formula:
$$4480 = 64 \times h$$

steps.title: Solve for height
steps.content: Divide both sides by 64: $$h = \frac{4480}{64} = 70 \text{ units}$$ That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
